response.justification: The candidate lacks direct experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, or deep-learning workload analysis—core requirements for the Principal Performance Architect role. Their background is primarily in full-stack web and embedded software development, with no evidence of hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, or performance modeling for AI accelerators, despite extensive software engineering experience.
strengths: Strong full-stack software engineering background with diverse language proficiency (TypeScript, DLang, C++, etc.)
Demonstrated experience in performance-critical systems and low-latency applications
Active open-source contributor with tools for testing, CI, and system-level development
concerns: No evidence of SoC, IP, or AI accelerator hardware modeling experience
Lacks background in deep learning workload optimization or GPU architecture
Education is a BS in CS, but no indication of 8+ years in hardware/performance-critical domains as required

key_factors.factor: Lack of AI accelerator hardware experience
Misalignment with required SoC/IP modeling skills
Strong software engineering and open-source contributions
